architecture_fit.note: Product of dot products (a*b) yields clean AND numerically (canonical sharpness ~1.0), but it is a bare analytic bilinear with no link to base_model.py, no attention head, and no documented delta.

baseline_comparison.note: Framework-supplied linear baseline shows a clear lift (canonical lift 0.49, sharpness 1.0 vs 0.51), but the attempt adds no explicit strawman or discussion of its own.

faithfulness.note: Purely analytic function — no model, no ablation or causal patching, and no README acknowledging the gap or proposing a check.

operating_range.note: Cosine axis 0.0-0.9 swept with graceful degradation (robustness 0.74), but no input-scale sweep across orders of magnitude.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is entirely hand-written (product probe) rather than learned, a hand-set AND circuit, though trivially simple.

visual_judgement.note: app.py fails the boot-check: 'ageneric' import typo, missing numpy import, and misused benchmark_panel — the demo does not load.

visualisation_rationale.note: No attempt README exists, so there is no 'Why this visualisation' rationale to assess.

notes: The product-probe model_fn produces strong automated metrics (canonical AND-sharpness ~1.0, clean lift over the linear baseline, robustness 0.74), so the headline numbers are healthy. But it provides no causal/faithfulness evidence (no model or ablation), no attempt README, and the Gradio app fails to boot due to an import typo and misused panel API. Worst of architecture/baseline/faithfulness (faithfulness=1) drives an overall fail.
